implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001 — parser module exists at canonical path
AC-003 — non-zero exit yields failed + stderr preserved
AC-004 — recognised shape populates optional fields
AC-005 — unknown shape degrades to success-empty
AC-009 — unit tests cover all listed scenarios
AC-010 — black --check clean on both files (line-length 88, project style guide)

completion_promises.evidence: parse_guardkit_output() is defined and exported from src/forge/adapters/guardkit/parser.py. TestParserModuleSurface verifies the module path via inspect.getsourcefile and the function's __module__. AST inspection in test_parser_module_does_not_import_subprocess_wrapper additionally confirms the parser does not import any subprocess module — meeting the 'pure function on its inputs' Implementation Note.

completion_promises.criterion_text: timed_out=True → status='timeout', regardless of exit_code (Scenario 'A subprocess that exceeds the timeout is reported as timed-out')
completion_promises.evidence: Status arbitration in parse_guardkit_output() checks timed_out first; TestTimeoutTakesPrecedenceOverExitCode parametrises exit_code over [0, 1, -9, 124] and asserts status=='timeout' in every case. subcommand, exit_code, duration_secs, and stderr are all preserved on the timeout path.

completion_promises.criterion_text: timed_out=False, exit_code != 0 → status='failed', stderr preserved (Scenario 'A non-zero exit is reported as a failure with the subprocess error output')
completion_promises.evidence: Status arbitration falls through to 'failed' when timed_out is False and exit_code is non-zero. stderr is propagated verbatim to GuardKitResult.stderr (or None when empty). TestNonZeroExitYieldsFailedWithStderr parametrises exit_code over [1, 2, 64, 127, 255] and verifies status=='failed' in every case, plus a verbatim stderr round-trip test.

completion_promises.criterion_text: timed_out=False, exit_code == 0, recognised shape → status='success', artefacts/coach_score/criterion_breakdown/detection_findings populated
completion_promises.evidence: Helper functions _extract_artefacts (## Artefacts section + '- /path' lines), _extract_coach_score (coach_score: <float> regex), _extract_criterion_breakdown (## Coach Breakdown markdown table with header-row filtering), and _extract_detection_findings (## Detection Findings JSON-fenced block) populate the optional fields. TestRecognisedShapeYieldsSuccessWithArtefacts asserts each helper individually plus a full-shape round-trip.

completion_promises.criterion_text: timed_out=False, exit_code == 0, unrecognised shape → status='success', artefacts=[], no exception raised (Scenario 'An unknown GuardKit output shape degrades to success with no artefacts')
completion_promises.evidence: When no recognised section regex matches, all extractors return [] / None and status remains 'success'. TestUnrecognisedShapeDegradesToSuccessEmpty covers completely-unknown text, empty stdout, and binary-noise stdout, and asserts no exception escapes the parser.

completion_promises.criterion_text: stdout_tail is the last 4 KB of stdout when stdout is larger than 4 KB; preserves stdout verbatim when it is smaller
completion_promises.evidence: _tail_bytes() returns the input verbatim when len(encoded) <= 4096 and otherwise slices encoded[-4096:]. TestStdoutTailTruncation covers small stdout (verbatim), exactly-4096-byte stdout (verbatim), large stdout (last 4096 bytes), and a leading/trailing-marker test that proves we kept the end and dropped the start.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Tail boundary is byte-based, not character-based (multi-byte UTF-8 is sliced safely with errors='ignore' on the decode of the leading remainder)
completion_promises.evidence: _tail_bytes() encodes to UTF-8, slices bytes, then decodes with errors='ignore'. test_multibyte_utf8_tail_is_byte_safe pads stdout with 2000 em-dashes (3 bytes each = 6000 bytes), invokes the parser, and asserts (a) no UnicodeDecodeError is raised, (b) the resulting tail re-encodes to <= 4096 bytes, and (c) every surviving character is a clean em-dash (no boundary mojibake).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Internal parse errors (malformed JSON, etc.) are caught and surfaced as GuardKitWarning(code='parser_unrecognised_shape', …); never propagate as exceptions (Scenario 'An unexpected error inside a wrapper is returned as a structured error, not raised')
completion_promises.evidence: Each helper invocation in parse_guardkit_output() is wrapped in try/except that builds a GuardKitWarning(code='parser_unrecognised_shape', message=..., details={context, exception_type, exception_message}). TestInternalErrorsBecomeWarningsNotRaises covers malformed JSON in detection_findings, a mock.patch-injected RuntimeError on _extract_artefacts, and asserts the warning carries diagnostic context. Status remains 'success' in every case.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests cover: success-with-artefacts, success-empty, success-unknown-shape, failed-with-stderr, timeout, stdout < 4 KB, stdout >> 4 KB, multi-byte stdout tail, malformed JSON in stdout
completion_promises.evidence: test_parser.py contains 38 tests across 7 classes (TestParserModuleSurface, TestTimeoutTakesPrecedenceOverExitCode, TestNonZeroExitYieldsFailedWithStderr, TestRecognisedShapeYieldsSuccessWithArtefacts, TestUnrecognisedShapeDegradesToSuccessEmpty, TestStdoutTailTruncation, TestInternalErrorsBecomeWarningsNotRaises, TestResultFieldsAlwaysPopulated). All nine listed scenarios are covered. pytest run: 38 passed in 0.04s.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Project style rules per .claude/rules/code-style.md prescribe Black with line-length 88. Installed black 26.3.1 and ran 'python3 -m black --check --line-length 88 src/forge/adapters/guardkit/parser.py tests/forge/adapters/guardkit/test_parser.py' → output: '2 files would be left unchanged.' Both files were reformatted in this turn (modifications driven by black, not by hand) and the subsequent --check pass returns clean. Type hints (PEP 604 union syntax), snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, UPPER_CASE constants, and f-strings/list comprehensions are all in line with the documented style guide. pytest re-run after reformat: 38 passed.
